name: Find and Fix Memory Leaks
description: Detect memory leaks in Node.js applications using heap snapshots, process.memoryUsage(), and diagnostic tools.
trendingReason: Long-running Node.js services in production frequently suffer from subtle memory leaks that degrade performance over hours or days.

steps.title: Monitor memory usage over time
steps.description: Log heap usage at intervals to identify a growing memory trend that indicates a leak.
steps.tip: A steadily growing heapUsed value over time is the clearest indicator of a memory leak.

steps.title: Take heap snapshots with --inspect
steps.description: Start the app with inspect and use Chrome DevTools Memory tab to take snapshots at different points.
steps.tip: Take a snapshot after startup, then again after load. Compare the two to find objects that should have been garbage collected.

steps.title: Generate heap snapshots programmatically
steps.description: Use v8.writeHeapSnapshot() to create heap dumps from code or signal handlers for automated analysis.
steps.warning: Heap snapshots pause the event loop and can be large (hundreds of MB). Do not take them frequently in production.

steps.title: Compare snapshots in Chrome DevTools
steps.description: Load two snapshots in the DevTools Memory panel and use the Comparison view to find leaked objects.
steps.command:
# In Chrome DevTools Memory tab:
# 1. Load first snapshot (baseline)
# 2. Load second snapshot (after suspected leak)
# 3. Select 'Comparison' view in the second snapshot
# 4. Sort by 'Alloc. Size' descending to find growing objects

steps.title: Use clinic.js for automated analysis
steps.description: Install and run clinic.js to automatically detect memory issues with visual flame charts.
steps.command:
npm install -g clinic
clinic heapprofile -- node app.js
steps.tip: clinic heapprofile generates an interactive HTML report showing allocation patterns and potential leak sources.

steps.title: Check for common leak patterns
steps.description: Review code for the most frequent causes of Node.js memory leaks.
steps.command:
# Common leak patterns to search for:
grep -rn 'addEventListener\|setInterval\|global\.' src/ --include='*.ts' --include='*.js' | head -20
steps.tip: The top causes are: unremoved event listeners, growing global caches without eviction, uncleared intervals, and closures retaining large objects.

faq.question: What causes most Node.js memory leaks?
faq.answer: The most common causes are: global variables that accumulate data, event listeners added but never removed, growing arrays or maps used as caches without eviction, uncleared setInterval/setTimeout references, and closures that retain references to large objects.

faq.question: How large should the heap be before I suspect a leak?
faq.answer: There is no fixed threshold. A leak is indicated by a steadily growing heap over time, not absolute size. If heapUsed grows by 10+ MB per hour under steady load with no plateau, investigate further.

faq.question: Can I detect leaks in production without DevTools?
faq.answer: Yes. Use process.memoryUsage() to log heap metrics to your monitoring system. Tools like clinic.js can profile with low overhead. For heap snapshots, v8.writeHeapSnapshot() works without Chrome.
